event: had given birth to a 3.1-kg baby girl in the 37th week of her first pregnancy
spontaneous vaginal delivery with an episiotomy
severe pain, mainly in the region of her buttocks
shortness of breath of sudden onset
worsening pain over her right thigh
sought treatment at the emergency department of a district hospital
oxygen saturation of 56% on room air
tachycardia with a pulse was 121 bpm
resuscitation with 30 mL/kg of normal saline
persistently hypotensive with a blood pressure of 56/30 mmHg
120 mL/kg (6 L) of crystalloid was given
persistently hypotensive post-intubation despite fluid resuscitation
extensive blistering ecchymotic patches over her right thigh extending to her right buttock
necrotizing fasciitis of the right thigh
high vaginal swab for culture and sensitivity taken
orthopedic and surgical opinions were sought
heart chambers were not grossly dilated
not suggest a massive pulmonary embolism
not suggest cardiomyopathy that can cause hemodynamic instability
transferred to an intensive care unit
high vaginal swab Gram positive cocci in chains on staining
blister fluid Gram positive cocci in chains on staining
skin lesion spread over her bilateral upper and lower limbs
blistering of the bilateral lower limbs that extended to her right lower abdomen
similar lesion on her right upper limb that extended to her right elbow
blood cultures taken at our centre had a negative yield
high vaginal swab grew S. pyogenes
blister fluid culture grew S. pyogenes
blood culture taken at the district hospital prior to the transfer was positive for group A beta hemolytic streptococcus
group A streptococcal toxic shock syndrome with necrotizing fasciitis
IV crystalline penicillin G was given ever four hours
a skin biopsy was not performed
cause of death determined to be septic shock with tissue necrosis and toxic shock syndrome secondary to S. pyogenes
